key_insight: Replacing half of Ni with Mn in Cu2NiXY4 kesterite chalcogenides preserves the tetragonal crystal structure but systematically narrows the bandgap through d-p orbital hybridization.
engineering_relevance: This suggests Mn doping is a practical tuning knob for designing kesterite semiconductors with adjustable bandgaps for optoelectronic devices.
method: First-principles DFT study of Cu2NiXY4 (X = Sn, Ge, Si; Y = S, Se) compounds
Used the mBJ+U approach to improve bandgap prediction accuracy
Modeled 50% Ni→Mn substitution in the kesterite unit cell due to crystallographic constraints
evidence_signal: Across the full family, the bandgap decreased from 1.028–3.397 eV to 1.007–3.333 eV, with Cu2NiSnS4 dropping from 1.59 eV to 1.49 eV.
watch_item: The reported substitution level is 50% in the unit cell, so lower Mn concentrations would require supercell modeling and may change the trends.
paper_title: First-principles Study of Structural and Electronic Properties of Mn-doped Cu2NiXY4 (X=Sn, Ge, Si; Y=S, Se) Chalcogenide Semiconductors
